description: Legal documents from 17th century Egypt, regarding a widow named Riqa (ריקא), the daughter of Ḥanan She'eltiel. The document on the verso is dated 3 Kislev 5438 AM (1677 CE). Recto: Upper section from a deed of sale for an apartment on Darb al-Muballaṭ that Riqa purchased from Avraham Malkhi for 3,000 medin. The upper lines of the document detail the succession of previous owners as well as the various scribes and judges who issued and/or validated the relevant documents from when the property exchanged hands. Verso: Deed of sale involving the same parties and property as the recto. Avraham Malkhi buys back the apartment on Darb al-Muballaṭ from Riqa for 3,000 medin. It is unclear how much time passed between the initial sale and resale of the property. Two scribes are listed: Nissim De Aragon and Moshe Shomer. The latter scribe may be the same person that is mentioned five years later on BL OR 6356.1 (PGPID 40943). MCD.
description_he: מסמך משפטי בAramaic, Hebrew. מוחזק בהספרייה הבריטית.
